differential_diagnoses: Hypersensitivity pneumonitis with severe pulmonary hypertension
next_steps: Genetic testing for EIF2AK4 mutations (associated with PVOD/PCH)
Surgical lung biopsy or transbronchial cryobiopsy if clinically stable enough to tolerate
Review of pathology specimens for signs of venous or capillary remodeling
Escalation of bridge therapies such as parenteral prostacyclin or mechanical circulatory support (e.g., ECMO) if hemodynamic decline continues
Reviewing for subtle signs of occult connective tissue disease
Prioritize urgent listing and evaluation for bilateral lung transplantation
Transition from pulmonary vasodilators to targeted inotropic support (e.g., milrinone or dobutamine) to stabilize the right ventricle
Expert thoracic radiology review of high-resolution CT for signs of PVOD (septal lines, centrilobular opacities, lymphadenopathy)
Avoid open lung biopsy due to prohibitive mortality risk in severe pulmonary hypertension
Consider screening for occult malignancy or PTTM via focused imaging (e.g., abdomen/pelvis CT)
Careful fluid management with diuretics to minimize venous congestion while maintaining right ventricular filling pressures
